source: Municipalities of Singapore, Penang and Malacca liabilities__CO273:668:16
filename: Municipalities of Singapore, Penang and Malacca liabilities__CO273:668:16.txt
summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** Colonial Office handling of **financial liabilities and payments** connected with the **Municipalities of Singapore, Penang and Malacca** during the **war with Japan**, including pensions, provident fund balances, allowances to dependants, and related banking procedures.  
- **Geography/time period:** Concerns **Malaya/Straits Settlements (Singapore, Penang/Prai)** with administration and payments routed through **London** and overseas points (**Australia/Sydney, India/Bombay, South Africa, New Zealand/Wellington**); main correspondence spans **1941–1943** (notably many items in **1942**).  
- **Key actors/organizations:** **Colonial Office** (Under-Secretary of State; officials incl. **J. J. Paskin**, **M. W. Dodds**, **W. B. L. Monson**), municipal agents **Messrs. Peirce & Williams** (incl. **G. R. Allen**, **W. Marsh**), **Chartered Bank of India, Australia & China**, **Malayan Government Agent (Sydney)**, **High Commissioner in Australia (Canberra)**, and **Trading with the Enemy Department (T.E.D.)**; later **Crown Agents** assume responsibility.  
- **Major decisions/proposals/events:**  
  - Proposal/arrangements to **continue and regulate allowances to dependants** of municipal employees (often missing/POWs), with attempts to set terminal dates (e.g., **to end Jan 1943**) and requests for **Colonial Office covering authority** to the bank.  
  - Efforts to **enable disbursement from municipal funds held in London** despite lack of documentation and imprest limits; Colonial Office indicates willingness to support agents/bank actions and “use good offices” with municipal authorities later.  
  - **Provident Fund payments**: agents compile “prima facie” lists and seek bank comments before Colonial Office approval; **Penang records clear only to 30 June 1940**, Singapore records to **31 Dec 1941** but “not in good condition,” leading to recommended delay for most Singapore payments.  
  - Agreement in principle to **continue paying life insurance premiums** for certain staff (including those still in Malaya/POWs), with later note that such payments were agreed as in order where policies were deposited and paid from the Provident Fund prior to **1942** (wording partly unclear).  
- **Notable individual cases:**  
  - **Mr. G. D. Baker** (Penang Municipal Electric Power Station, Prai): proposal to terminate service **from 31 Dec 1941**; salary **$160/month**; suggested payment **3 months’ salary (£56 sterling mentioned in a draft telegram; another letter suggests “3 months’ salary amounting to 356 sterling” but this figure is unclear/possibly OCR error)**; Provident Fund credit **$1,585.22 (to 30 June 1940)**.  
  - **Mr. T. Rogers** (Penang Municipal engineer/electrical engineer): request/authorization discussed for **10% payment under Provident Fund Additional Rule 4**; agents estimate pension based on **Straits £74,682.14** balance at **30 June 1940**, giving **£871.5.9 sterling per annum** (parity basis); later telegram notes payment authorized was made but treated as **interest on Provident Fund balance**, not pension.  
  - **Mr. A. J. Brown** (Penang Fire Brigade Superintendent): disputes retirement/partial Provident Fund payment; claims **8 months 15 days leave pay due**, **$175 transport allowance arrears**, and refuses “part” Provident Fund settlement; High Commissioner notes he was paid salary up to **25 Sept 1942**.  
  - **Mrs. Byrne** (Singapore): instructed payment via Chartered Bank at **Bombay** for **6 months** (amount appears inconsistently as **£5** or **£15** per month due to OCR/duplicate drafts).  
- **Notable numbers/dates:** Municipal funds and securities mentioned include **~£17,000** current/deposit balances in London and **~£840,000 nominal** sterling securities; Penang municipal debenture loans outstanding **$15,875,000**, with **just under $500,000** having contactable addresses (per analysis). Dependants’ payments: recommendation to transfer **£4,000 sterling** to the Malayan Agent (Sydney) as funds expected to be exhausted by end of year (**Dec 1942**). Exchange rate recommended for settlements: **2/4d to the dollar**. Lists of Singapore Provident Fund totals show **£203,784.81 contributions** and **£196,042.19 donations** (as presented in the attached schedule).
